title: Substrate Adapter Descriptor v1
description: Self-description emitted by every substrate adapter. Validated by `cognitive-os bridge substrate verify`. Declaring unsupported capabilities is worse than declaring none; the kernel uses this descriptor to route operations and skip those the substrate cannot honor.

version.description: Semver of the adapter implementation (not the substrate it wraps).

substrate.description: Identity of the substrate this adapter wraps.

capabilities.description: What the substrate can honor. Absent or false means the operation is unsupported.

preserve_provenance.description: True iff the substrate stores memory-contract provenance fields losslessly.

scope_keys.description: Which ScopeMap keys the substrate honors natively. Keys not listed are stored in metadata, not used for filtering.

config_schema.description: JSON schema describing adapter-specific configuration (API keys, base URLs, local paths).

lossy_fields.description: Memory-contract fields the substrate cannot preserve. Flagged so operators know what degrades when routing through this adapter.
